description:
Search within a Unity scene or prefab hierarchy and return precise object paths for later unity_yaml_read calls.

For .unity/.prefab files, this tool reads live Unity Editor state through the locus_unity plugin first so unsaved scene or prefab changes are visible, then falls back to direct text YAML parsing when the Editor path is unavailable. Use this when you know a name, component, tag/layer clue, prefab source, serialized field clue, or subtree but need the exact object path. Results preserve important list-view information inline on each result row: full path, component names, active/static state, Tag, and Layer. Prefab source paths and serialized field matches participate in query matching when selected but are omitted from result rows. When sibling names repeat, paths use a 1-based segment ordinal such as `Root/Enemy[2]`.

`query` searches the fields selected by `match_fields`. By default it searches object paths, object names, component names, annotations, tags, layers, and resolved prefab source paths. It defaults to case-insensitive substring matching; prefix with `re:` for a case-insensitive regular expression. Include `field_name` and/or `field_value` in `match_fields` to search serialized component fields. Field matching is more expensive on large scenes; use it with `path_prefix` when possible. `component_filter` supports one component name or comma-separated component names for OR matching. If both query and component_filter are provided, a result must satisfy both.
- When Headroom is enabled and output exceeds the compress threshold, large search output is compressed through headroom-ai (requires headroom proxy or HEADROOM_API_KEY).

file_path.description: Path to the Unity .unity or .prefab YAML file, relative to the project root (e.g. 'Assets/Scenes/Main.unity') or absolute path.

query.description: Optional text search over object paths, names, component names, annotations, and resolved prefab source paths. Use 're:<pattern>' for a case-insensitive regular expression.

component_filter.description: Optional component filter. Use a component name such as 'Light' or 'Canvas', or comma-separated component names for OR matching.

match_fields.description: Optional comma-separated query scope. Supported values: path, name, component, annotation, tag, layer, prefab_source, field_name, field_value, default, all. Omit to use default matching. Use field_name or field_value only when you need serialized component field matching.

path_prefix.description: Optional hierarchy path prefix for searching one subtree, such as 'Environment/Lighting' or a returned duplicate-safe path like 'Root/Enemy[2]'.

limit.description: Optional maximum number of search results to print. Defaults to 50.
